content.claim: NGO advocacy sources estimate that two-thirds of Roma children in Hungary are taught separately in segregated schools, classes, or special institutions, and that a large proportion of Roma children are misclassified as mildly intellectually disabled, making this a major mechanism of educational segregation, yet these figures are unaudited advocacy estimates rather than official statistics.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Estimates are not derived from independent audit or official data collection
No methodology for the two-thirds figure is provided in available sources
The claim is consistent with ECtHR case law patterns but not independently verified through system-wide data
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
